session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 36 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 114 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

91   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
92   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
93 } migration_state_t;

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Become the host (called when elected as future host).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2512 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2512                                                                               {
2513   if (!session) {
2514     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2515     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2516   }
2517 
2518   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2519            session->participant_id[1]);
2520 
2521   // Get configured port (or use default)
2522   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2523 
2524   // Mark ourselves as host
2525   session->is_host = true;
2526 
2527   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2528   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2529     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2530         .port = host_port,
2531         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2532         .max_clients = 32,
2533         .encryption_enabled = true,
2534     };
2535 
2536     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2537     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2538       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2539       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2540     }
2541 
2542     // Start listening for new participant connections
2543     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2544     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2545       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2546       return hstart;
2547     }
2548 
2549     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2550   }
2551 
2552   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2553   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2554   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2555   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2556   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2557   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2558   // Set connection type based on NAT quality (for now use DIRECT_PUBLIC for localhost dev environments)
2559   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2560 
2561   if (session->acds_transport) {
2562     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2563                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2564     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2565              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2566   }
2567 
2568   // Mark migration complete
2569   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2570 
2571   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2572   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2573 
2574   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2575 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2389 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2389                                                                                    {
2390   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2391     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2392     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2393   }
2394 
2395   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2396   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2397     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2398     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2399       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2400     }
2401     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2402     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2403   }
2404 
2405   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2406   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2407   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2408     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2409   }
2410 
2411   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2412   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2413     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2414   }
2415 
2416   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2417 
2418   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2419   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2420     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2421     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2422       // Ping timed out
2423       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2424       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2425       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2426                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2427 
2428       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2429       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2430         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2431         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2432       }
2433     }
2434   }
2435 
2436   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2437   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2438   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2439     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2440     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2441     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2442       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2443       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2444         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2445         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2446         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2447                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2448       } else {
2449         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2450         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2451         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2452           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2453           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2454         }
2455       }
2456     }
2457   }
2458 
2459   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2460 }

2577                                                                                          {
2578   if (!session) {
2579     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2580     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2581   }
2582 
2583   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2584            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2585 
2586   // Destroy old participant context if present
2587   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2588     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2589     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2590   }
2591 
2592   // Create new participant context for the future host
2593   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2594       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2595       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2596       .enable_audio = true,
2597       .enable_video = true,
2598       .encryption_enabled = true,
2599   };
2600 
2601   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2602   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2603     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2604     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2605   }
2606 
2607   // Attempt connection to future host
2608   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2609   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2610     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2611     return pconn;
2612   }
2613 
2614   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2615 
2616   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2617   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2618   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2619   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2620 
2621   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2622            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2623 
2624   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2625   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2626 
2627   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2628   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2629 
2630   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2631 }

  discovery_config_t * config)


Create a new discovery session.


Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error


Definition at line 52 of file

52                                                                                 {
53   if (!config) {
54     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
55     return NULL;
56   }
57 
58   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
59   if (!session) {
60     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
61     return NULL;
62   }
63 
64   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
65   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
66   session->acds_transport = NULL;
67   session->peer_manager = NULL;
68   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
69   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
70   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
71   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
72   session->stun_servers = NULL;
73   session->stun_count = 0;
74   session->turn_servers = NULL;
75   session->turn_count = 0;
76 
77   // Initialize host liveness detection
78   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
79   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
80   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
81   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
82   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
83   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
84   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
85 
86   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
87            session->host_ctx);
88 
89   // Load or generate identity key
90   char identity_path[256];
91   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
92   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
93     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
94     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
95       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
96       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
97       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
99         // Save for future use
100         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
101       }
102     }
103   }
104 
105   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
106     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
107     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
108     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
109   } else {
110     char fingerprint[65];
111     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
112     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
113   }
114 
115   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
116   const options_t *opts = options_get();
117   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
118     // Load all identity keys from options
119     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
120       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
121       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
122       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
123       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
124         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
125         char fingerprint[65];
126         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
127         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
128       } else {
129         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
130       }
131     }
132     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
133     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
134   } else {
135     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
136     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
137     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
138   }
139 
140   // ACDS connection info
141   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
143   }
144   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
145     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
146   } else {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
148   }
149   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
150 
151   // Session string (if joining)
152   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
153     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
154     session->is_initiator = false;
155   } else {
156     session->is_initiator = true;
157   }
158 
159   // Callbacks
160   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
161   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
162   session->on_error = config->on_error;
163   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
164   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
165   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
166 
167   // Initialize mutex for NAT quality protection
168   if (mutex_init(&session->nat_quality_mutex, "discovery_nat_quality") != 0) {
169     log_warn("Failed to initialize NAT quality mutex");
170   }
171 
172   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
173             session->acds_port);
174 
175   return session;
176 }

178                                                              {
179   if (!session) {
180     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
181     return;
182   }
183 
184   // Close ACDS connection
185   if (session->acds_transport) {
186     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
187     session->acds_transport = NULL;
188   }
189   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
190     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
191     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
192   }
193 
194   // Destroy host context if active
195   if (session->host_ctx) {
196     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
197     session->host_ctx = NULL;
198   }
199 
200   // Destroy participant context if active
201   if (session->participant_ctx) {
202     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
203     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
204   }
205 
206   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
207   if (session->peer_manager) {
208     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
209     session->peer_manager = NULL;
210   }
211 
212   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
213   if (session->stun_servers) {
214     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
215     session->stun_servers = NULL;
216     session->stun_count = 0;
217   }
218 
219   if (session->turn_servers) {
220     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
221     session->turn_servers = NULL;
222     session->turn_count = 0;
223   }
224 
225   // Wait for NAT detection thread to complete if still running
226   if (session->nat_detection_thread_started) {
227     log_debug("Waiting for NAT detection thread to complete...");
228     asciichat_thread_join(&session->nat_detection_thread, NULL);
229     session->nat_detection_thread_started = false;
230   }
231 
232   // Destroy NAT quality mutex
233   mutex_destroy(&session->nat_quality_mutex);
234 
235   SAFE_FREE(session);
236 }

  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2462 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2462                                                                                                                      {
2463   if (!session) {
2464     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2465     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2466   }
2467 
2468   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2469 
2470   // Initialize migration context
2471   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2472   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2473   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2474   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2475 
2476   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2477   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2478   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2479   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2480   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2481   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2482   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2483 
2484   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2485 
2486   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2487   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2488     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2489     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2490     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2491     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2492     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2493   }
2494 
2495   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2496   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2497   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2498 
2499   // Check if I am the future host
2500   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2501     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2502     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2503     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2504   } else {
2505     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2506     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2507              session->ring.future_host_port);
2508     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2509   }
2510 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1632 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1632                                                                                               {
1633   if (!session) {
1634     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1635   }
1636 
1637   // Handle state-specific processing
1638   switch (session->state) {
1639   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1640     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1641     // Note: don't use a hard timeout here - let the outer process timeout (snapshot delay) control max wait
1642     // The discovery_run() loop will keep calling this until session becomes active or process exits
1643     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
1644     uint64_t wait_duration = now_ns - session->waiting_peer_start_ns;
1645 
1646     log_debug("★ WAITING_PEER: duration=%.3fs (no timeout, waiting for peer...)", wait_duration / 1e9);
1647 
1648     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1649       // Do not set socket timeout - let receive_packet_secure use its own timeout
1650       // Setting a short socket timeout conflicts with its hardcoded 1-second timeout
1651       packet_type_t type;
1652       void *data = NULL;
1653       size_t len = 0;
1654       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1655 
1656       // Try to receive a packet
1657       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1658           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1659       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1660         if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1661           log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1662           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1663           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1664         } else {
1665           // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1666           log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1667           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1668         }
1669       } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1670         // Timeout is normal, just return
1671       } else {
1672         log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1673       }
1674     } else {
1675       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1676       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1677     }
1678     break;
1679   }
1680 
1681   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1682     // Handle NAT negotiation
1683     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1684       // Initialize negotiation
1685       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1686       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1687 
1688       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS so peers can evaluate us
1689       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1690       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1691         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1692       }
1693 
1694       // ACDS relays NETWORK_QUALITY only to OTHER participants, not back to sender
1695       // For now in discovery mode, assume we're the only one and become host immediately
1696       // A real implementation would wait for peer to join via PARTICIPANT_JOINED, then connect directly
1697       log_info("Proceeding as host (ACDS cannot relay peer quality in solo mode)");
1698       session->negotiate.our_quality.detection_complete = true;
1699       session->negotiate.peer_quality_received = true;
1700       memset(&session->negotiate.peer_quality, 0, sizeof(session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1701       // Mark peer quality as detected but with worst NAT type so we win host election
1702       session->negotiate.peer_quality.detection_complete = true;
1703       session->negotiate.peer_quality.nat_type = ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC;
1704     }
1705 
1706     // Check for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS (in case we're waiting for host discovery)
1707     if (session->acds_transport && !session->is_initiator && !session->host_address[0]) {
1708       packet_type_t type;
1709       void *data = NULL;
1710       size_t len = 0;
1711       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1712 
1713       asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1714       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT) {
1715         acip_host_announcement_t *announcement = (acip_host_announcement_t *)data;
1716         log_info("Received HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS: %s:%u", announcement->host_address, announcement->host_port);
1717         SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, announcement->host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1718         session->host_port = announcement->host_port;
1719         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1720         // Continue to host role determination below
1721       } else if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1722         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1723       }
1724     }
1725 
1726     // Determine host role (now that peer_quality is set)
1727     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1728       log_info("Determining host role based on NAT qualities...");
1729       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1730       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1731         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1732 
1733         if (session->is_host) {
1734           // We become the host
1735           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1736                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1737                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1738           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1739           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1740           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1741 
1742           // Fallback to localhost if NAT detection failed to determine address
1743           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1744             log_warn("NAT address empty, using localhost fallback");
1745             SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->host_address));
1746           }
1747 
1748           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1749         } else {
1750           // They are host (shouldn't happen in solo mode, but handle it)
1751           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1752                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1753                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1754                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1755           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1756           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1757 
1758           // If host address is empty, wait for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS instead of using fallback
1759           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1760             log_info("Waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS to discover host address...");
1761             // Stay in NEGOTIATING state to continue waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT
1762             break;
1763           }
1764 
1765           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1766         }
1767       } else {
1768         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1769         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1770       }
1771     }
1772     break;
1773   }
1774 
1775   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1776     // Start hosting
1777     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1778       // Create host context with configured port
1779       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1780 
1781       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1782           .port = host_port,
1783           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1784           .max_clients = 32,
1785           .encryption_enabled = true,
1786       };
1787 
1788       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1789       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1790         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1791         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1792         break;
1793       }
1794 
1795       // Start listening
1796       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1797       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1798         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1799         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1800         break;
1801       }
1802 
1803       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1804     }
1805 
1806     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1807     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1808       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1809     }
1810 
1811     // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so participants can discover us
1812     int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1813     acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
1814     memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1815     memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->host_id, 16);
1816     SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
1817     announcement.host_port = host_port;
1818     announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
1819 
1820     if (session->acds_transport) {
1821       packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
1822                                 sizeof(announcement), 0);
1823       log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS at %s:%u", announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
1824     }
1825 
1826     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1827     break;
1828   }
1829 
1830   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1831     // Connect to host as participant
1832 
1833     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1834     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1835       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1836       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1837         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1838         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1839         break;
1840       }
1841 
1842       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1843       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1844         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1845                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1846 
1847         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1848         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1849             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1850 
1851         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1852           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1853           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1854           break;
1855         }
1856 
1857         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1858         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1859         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1860       }
1861 
1862       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1863       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1864         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1865         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1866 
1867         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1868           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1869                     timeout_ms);
1870 
1871           // Check if we have retries remaining
1872           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1873           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1874             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1875             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1876 
1877             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1878                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1879 
1880             // Wait for backoff period
1881             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1882 
1883             // Destroy old peer manager
1884             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1885             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1886 
1887             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1888             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1889             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1890               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1891               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1892 
1893               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1894               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1895                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1896                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1897                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1898               }
1899 
1900               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1901             }
1902 
1903             // Reset connection state for retry
1904             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1905             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1906 
1907             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1908                      max_attempts + 1);
1909           } else {
1910             // No more retries - connection failed
1911             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1912 
1913             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1914             char error_msg[256];
1915             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1916                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1917             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1918 
1919             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1920             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1921               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1922               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1923               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1924             }
1925 
1926             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1927           }
1928         }
1929       }
1930 
1931       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1932       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1933       if (session->acds_transport) {
1934         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1935         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1936         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1937         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1938 
1939         fd_set readfds;
1940         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1941         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1942 
1943         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1944         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1945           // Data available to read
1946           packet_type_t type;
1947           void *data = NULL;
1948           size_t len = 0;
1949           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1950 
1951           asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1952               packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1953 
1954           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1955             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1956             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1957             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1958           } else {
1959             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1960           }
1961         }
1962       }
1963 
1964       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1965       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1966       break;
1967     }
1968 
1969     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1970       // Create participant context for this session
1971       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1972           .address = session->host_address,
1973           .port = session->host_port,
1974           .enable_audio = true,
1975           .enable_video = true,
1976           .encryption_enabled = true,
1977       };
1978 
1979       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1980       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1981         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1982         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1983         break;
1984       }
1985 
1986       // Attempt connection
1987       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1988       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1989         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1990         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1991         break;
1992       }
1993 
1994       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1995 
1996       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1997       // Get the socket from participant context
1998       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1999       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2000         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
2001         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
2002         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
2003         break;
2004       }
2005 
2006       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
2007       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
2008       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
2009       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
2010       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
2011       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
2012       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
2013       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
2014       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
2015 
2016       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
2017       if (result != 0) {
2018         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
2019         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
2020         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
2021         break;
2022       }
2023 
2024       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
2025       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
2026       packet_type_t packet_type;
2027       void *payload = NULL;
2028       size_t payload_len = 0;
2029 
2030       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
2031       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
2032         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
2033         if (payload) {
2034           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
2035         }
2036         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
2037         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
2038         break;
2039       }
2040 
2041       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
2042         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
2043         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
2044         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
2045         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
2046         break;
2047       }
2048 
2049       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
2050       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
2051       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
2052 
2053       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
2054         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
2055         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
2056         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
2057         break;
2058       }
2059 
2060       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
2061                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
2062 
2063       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
2064       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
2065       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
2066       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
2067       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
2068       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
2069       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
2070       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
2071       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
2072       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
2073 
2074       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
2075       if (result != 0) {
2076         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
2077         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
2078         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
2079         break;
2080       }
2081 
2082       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
2083       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
2084     }
2085 
2086     // Check if connection is established
2087     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
2088     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
2089     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
2090               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
2091 
2092     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
2093       // Log more details about why it's not connected
2094       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
2095     }
2096 
2097     if (is_connected) {
2098       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
2099       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2100     } else {
2101       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
2102                 "is_connected=%d)",
2103                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
2104     }
2105     break;
2106   }
2107 
2108   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
2109     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
2110     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
2111       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
2112       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
2113 
2114       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
2115 
2116       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
2117         // Data available to read
2118         packet_type_t type;
2119         void *data = NULL;
2120         size_t len = 0;
2121         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
2122 
2123         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
2124             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
2125 
2126         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2127           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
2128           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
2129           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
2130         }
2131       }
2132     }
2133 
2134     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
2135     if (!session->is_host) {
2136       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
2137       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
2138       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2139         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
2140         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
2141 
2142         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
2143         const options_t *opts = options_get();
2144         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
2145           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
2146           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2147           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2148           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2149         }
2150 
2151         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
2152         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
2153         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
2154       }
2155     }
2156     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2157     break;
2158 
2159   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
2160     // Host migration in progress
2161     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
2162     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
2163       // Migration completed successfully
2164       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2165       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
2166     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
2167       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
2168       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
2169       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2170       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2171     }
2172     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2173     break;
2174 
2175   default:
2176     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
2177     break;
2178   }
2179 
2180   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2181 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1435 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1435                                                                         {
1436   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1437 
1438   if (!session) {
1439     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1440   }
1441 
1442   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1443            session->session_string);
1444 
1445   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1446   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1447     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1448     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1449   }
1450 
1451   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1452   // Connect to ACDS
1453   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1454   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1455     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1456     return result;
1457   }
1458   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1459 
1460   // Check again after connection
1461   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1462     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1463     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1464   }
1465 
1466   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1467   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1468   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1469   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1470 
1471   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1472   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1473     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1474     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1475     return result;
1476   }
1477 
1478   // Store identity key in handshake context
1479   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1480   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1481   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1482   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1483 
1484   // Step 0: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1485   {
1486     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1487     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1488     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1489     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1490 
1491     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1492     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1493       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1494       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1495       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1496                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1497       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1498     }
1499 
1500     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1501       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1502                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1503       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1504       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1505       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1506     }
1507 
1508     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1509     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1510     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1511 
1512     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1513     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1514     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1515     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1516       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1517       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1518       return result;
1519     }
1520     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1521   }
1522 
1523   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1524   {
1525     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1526     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1527     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1528     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1529 
1530     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1531     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1532       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1533       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1534       return result;
1535     }
1536 
1537     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1538     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1539 
1540     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1541       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1542       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1543       return result;
1544     }
1545   }
1546 
1547   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1548   {
1549     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1550     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1551     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1552     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1553 
1554     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1555     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1556       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1557       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1558       return result;
1559     }
1560 
1561     result =
1562         crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1563     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1564 
1565     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1566       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1567       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1568       return result;
1569     }
1570   }
1571 
1572   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1573   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1574   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1575   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1576     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1577     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1578     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1579     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1580 
1581     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1582     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1583       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1584       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1585       return result;
1586     }
1587 
1588     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1589     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1590 
1591     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1592       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1593       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1594       return result;
1595     }
1596   } else {
1597     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1598   }
1599 
1600   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1601 
1602   // Check again after handshake
1603   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1604     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1605     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1606   }
1607 
1608   // Create or join session
1609   if (session->is_initiator) {
1610     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1611     result = create_session(session);
1612     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1613              session->participant_ctx, result);
1614 
1615     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1616       // Initiator also needs to register itself as a participant so it can receive PARTICIPANT_JOINED notifications
1617       // when other peers join. This makes the initiator discoverable to other participants.
1618       log_info("Initiator: now registering self as participant in created session");
1619       result = join_session(session);
1620       log_info("Initiator: join_session result=%d", result);
1621     }
1622     return result;
1623   } else {
1624     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1625     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1626     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1627              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1628     return result;
1629   }
1630 }

Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new
    joiner).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success

2362     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");

2365

2370 
2371     // HOST: Run proactive host election
2372     // 1. Collect NETWORK_QUALITY from all connected participants (TODO: request fresh data)
2373     // 2. Measure the host's own NAT quality
2374     // 3. Run deterministic election to determine future host
2375     // 4. Broadcast FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED to all participants via ACDS

2377     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2378       log_error("Host election failed: %d", result);
2379       return result;
2380     }
2381

2383   } else {

Parameters


Session Session context

2185     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2186     return;
2187   }
2188 
2189   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
2190 
2191   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
2192   if (session->acds_transport && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
2193     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
2194     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
2195     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2196     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2197 
2198     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg),
2199                               0);
2200   }
2201 
2202   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED);
